expectations.text: Uses exchange_calendars library (xcals) rather than hand-rolling hour-range checks
expectations.evidence: Hand-rolls hour-range checks with pandas .dt.tz_convert() + .dt.hour/.dt.minute comparisons. No exchange_calendars import. Lines 62-83 are pure pandas vectorized math.

expectations.text: Uses vectorized approach (pre-computed sets + .isin(), or equivalent) rather than per-row .apply()
expectations.evidence: Fully vectorized using .dt.tz_convert(), .dt.weekday, .dt.hour*60+.dt.minute. No .apply() or per-row Python calls. Lines 62-83.

expectations.text: Correctly handles Tokyo lunch break (11:30-12:30 JST) — either via is_open_on_minute() or explicit exclusion
expectations.evidence: Lines 79-83: Explicit morning (540-690) and afternoon (750-900) windows with tyo_morning | tyo_afternoon, excluding 690-750 (11:30-12:30 JST).

expectations.text: DST is handled implicitly via IANA timezones (not manual UTC offset tables)
expectations.evidence: Uses .dt.tz_convert('America/New_York') etc. — pandas handles DST via IANA. No manual offset tables.

expectations.text: Output is a complete, runnable Python script (not pseudocode or partial snippets)
expectations.evidence: 146 lines, complete with imports, function, __main__ demo, and assertion-based smoke tests. Fully runnable.
